chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI CP NO.D-5028/2021 ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Date Order with signature of Judge ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 08.09.2021 Mr. Rafiq Ahmed Kalwar advocate for petitioners. Mr. Ali Safdar Depar, AAG................ Mr. Aamir Latif advocate files Vakalatnama and statement on behalf of NBP taken on record. Such statement is reproduced herewith:- “By and on behalf of the respondents No. 2 & 3 (M/S. National Bank of Pakistan), it is respectfully submitted as under:- 1. That the petitioners who have not opted for compromise / agreement / discharge in favor of NBP are covered by the MTO Scheme and are alike / identical to the respondents in C. A. No. 1644 / 2013 have been extended the same benefits as per judgment dated 21.09.2016 passed by the Apex Court in C. A. No. 1644 / 2013. 2. It is further submitted that the arrears are being paid in 03 installments instead of 06 installments as directed by the Hon'ble Supreme Court in the judgment referred hereinabove. 3. In addition to the above, it is further submitted that the final installment shall be made with the salary of December, 2021. 4. It is also submitted that if any petitioner feels any grievance in respect of his fixation may make representation to the competent authority of the Bank through proper channel for the redressal thereof. In view of the above statement this petition may be disposed of.” In view of statement filed on behalf of NPB, instant petition is disposed of with regard to promotion of petitioner that he would be at liberty to submit representation with concerned authority

It is also submitted that if any petitioner feels any grievance in respect of his fixation may make representation to the competent authority of the Bank through proper channel for the redressal thereof. In view of the above statement this petition may be disposed of.” In view of statement filed on behalf of NPB, instant petition is disposed of with regard to promotion of petitioner that he would be at liberty to submit representation with concerned authority - { 2 } - that shall decide the fate of that representation in light of the judgment passed by the apex court in Civil Appeal No.3435/2018. J U D G E J U D G E IK
